channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Mega Man Joins Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds This Month
item.content:encoded:
Mega Man is coming to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds.

From one blue mascot to another, Mega Man is the latest crossover downloadable content coming to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds. The Mega Man DLC will be released on March 25. The trailer was officially announced on Sonic the Hedgehog’s social media accounts.

The DLC pack adds Mega Man and Proto Man as selectable characters. Also, there’s the new Wily Castle course, the Rush Roadstar vehicle, and Mega Man’s theme song as well.

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds Provides a Hub for Major IP Crossovers

Other downloadable content packs for this game include SpongeBob SquarePants, Pac-Man, the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, Avatar: The Last Airbender, and Minecraft. The game was originally released in 2025 and received very high scores in the kart racing genre.

Mega Man is gearing up for his return in the upcoming 2027 Mega Man Dual Override game from Capcom. Learn more about that game at this link.

Sonic Continues to Be Sega’s Bread and Butter

Sega is continuing to maximize the Sonic the Hedgehog intellectual property. First released in 1991, Sonic quickly became the company’s mascot and has had highs and lows right alongside the Sega brand. The fourth Sonic the Hedgehog theatrical release is currently in production. Learn more about that movie and its cast at this link.

Learn more about the strategy of the Sega brand moving forward at this link.

Have you played CrossWorlds? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: LEC May Be Played Online Next Year, According To Claims
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games may move the LEC to exclusively online matches, outside of Roadtrips, from 2027 according to rumours currently circulating online.

While co-streaming the ongoing First Stand tournament, retired League of Legends pro Kim ‘Wadid’ Bae-in revealed he has heard that Riot is considering moving the LEC out of the usual Riot Games Arena studio in Berlin.

Riot hosts all LEC events at that said studio other than Roadtrips — live arena events hosted and organised by an LEC team — and the Season Finals, which is currently the only Riot-organised LAN event for the LEC each year.

And according to Wadid, these arena shows and Roadtrips would still happen, but the majority of the season would be played online.

Currently, the Berlin studio is shared between the LEC and another Riot-owned esports property in Valorant’s VCT EMEA league. It’s not known if this alleged change would affect the Valorant league or not.

LEC Cancels Live Events Rumour

Translated by a Redditor, what South Korean streamer Wadid allegedly said is:

“Why is the LEC in trouble? Starting next year, the Berlin studio might disappear. From 2027 onward, they might move every match online, completely shut down the Berlin studio, and there may no longer be any offline broadcasts going forward.”

The LEC has hosted online matches before, but only during the COVID-19 pandemic when live events were not allowed.

It’s possible that cancelling live shows would be taken as a cost-cutting measure by the League of Legends publisher.

Live esports events are expensive to run, hence why the NLC LoL tournament is currently without an organiser.

Riot has already been cutting costs for its esports leagues by hosting fewer arena shows and occasionally running game days without live audiences.

Wadid claims he heard this news from a “highly reliable source” and has also heard that some teams are already preparing for online matches.

Wadid added that while he doesn’t like the current set-up in Berlin, he has heard that Riot isn’t even considering opening up a venue in a new location.

For now, Riot has not made any official statement and from Wadid’s claims, it doesn’t sound like a decision has yet been made.

What do you think about the possibility of the LEC being moved to exclusively online? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, in more hopeful LoL esports news, Rekkles has announced he isn’t retiring.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Rocket League Esports World Cup 2026: All You Need To Know
item.content:encoded:
Later this summer, the Esports World Cup (EWC) will host the third iteration of its $1 million (£750k) Rocket League esports tournament of which more details have now been announced.

Last year’s event was won by Karmine Corp, who defeated fellow European team Geekay Esports in the final. The French organisation will now automatically qualify for this year’s event to defend their title.

Here is everything you need to know about the upcoming esports event in Saudi Arabia.

When Is The Rocket League Esports Cup 2026?

The Rocket League event of the Esports World Cup 2026 will take place from August 12 until August 16 during Week 6 of this year’s EWC.

This will be the penultimate week of the event, sharing the week with PUBG Mobile, Chess, and Rainbow Six Siege X.

Who Will Compete At the Rocket League Esports World Cup 2026?

As mentioned earlier, last year’s champions Karmine Corp automatically qualify for this year’s event. They will be joined by 15 other teams from around the world competing for a lion’s share of the lucrative $1m prize pool.

Who claims those remaining spots will be decided by the followiung criteria:

  • The top 3 teams on the RLCS Europe Points Ranking
  • The top 3 teams on the RLCS North America Points Ranking
  • The top 3 teams on the RLCS Middle East & North Africa Points Ranking
  • The top 2 teams on the RLCS South America Points Ranking
  • The top team on the RLCS Oceania Ranking
  • The top team on the RLCS Sub-Saharan Africa Ranking
  • The top team on the RLCS Asia-Pacific Ranking
  • The winner of the EWC Last Chance Qualifier+

Rocket League Championship Series (RLCS) Rankings are based on performances at regional Open tournaments and the international Majors with both offering points. Various iterations of such events are coming up, including the Paris Major in May.

Who do you think will win this year’s title? Share your predictions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And to keep up with latest Rocket League and Espotrs World Cup news by signing up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Double Fine’s Kiln Finally Gets April Release Date
item.content:encoded:
Nearly ten years later, Kiln from Double Fine Productions has finally received a release date. The game was first announced in 2017, born from Double Fine’s ‘Amnesia Fortnight’ event, an annual two-week game jam that produces prototype game ideas. The game remained mostly silent for a decade before re-emerging during the January 2026 Xbox Developer Direct.

Now, it’s been announced that the game will be released on April 23. Xbox confirmed the news in its most recent Xbox Wire post. The game is a multiplayer online Pottery party brawler. Interested players can sign up for an open Beta right now. The beta will take place on April 9-11.

More on Kiln

Originally announced in 2017, it was described as a multiplayer, team-based brawler by Derek Brand, featuring unique player-sculpted characters and dynamic, physics-based animation and destruction. Now, the game has a fully developed pottery-driven approach.

“In Kiln, you shape a small, medium, or large ball of clay into a vessel on a pottery wheel with surprisingly intuitive controls. The size and shape of the vessel determines its stats (like health and capacity for water) and special ability (with different abilities corresponding to shapes like bowls, chalices, bottles, etc.). Decorate with various glazes and cosmetic attachments (all for style), and you’re good to go. The matches comprise two teams of four pots competing to gather water and quench the other team’s kiln three times before the same is done to theirs, breaking enemy pots along the way in chaotic brawls,” the Xbox Wire post describes.

Notably, it was stated that there would be no voice chats integrated into the game itself. Derek Brand has explained that the studio wants to keep the game friendly for all ages.

Insider Gaming will continue to bring the latest updates on Kiln as the game approaches release. The game will be available on Xbox, PC, PlayStation 5, and Steam. Also available on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate.

Are you excited for the upcoming game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP
